name: Macro Photography for Beginners: A UK Starter's Guide

step.name: Pick one subject and commit to it
step.text: Choose a single macro subject — one flower, one feather, one specific insect — rather than trying to photograph the whole scene.

step.name: Set aperture, shutter, ISO in that order
step.text: Start at f/8 for depth of field, set shutter speed based on handheld vs tripod (1/500s minimum handheld), and let ISO rise to 800–1600 as needed.

step.name: Switch to manual focus
step.text: Set the lens to manual, rotate to your chosen magnification, then focus by moving your body closer or further from the subject.

step.name: Check what's behind the subject
step.text: A sharp subject against a messy background is a weak image. Shift position until the background is clean and ideally darker than the subject.

step.name: Rock gently, fire in bursts
step.text: For handheld macro, rock gently back and forth at the waist while firing three to five frame bursts. Shallow depth of field means micro-movements bring different parts into focus.

step.name: Check the screen, adjust, repeat
step.text: Zoom into the LCD after each burst. Adjust one variable (aperture, shutter, position) and shoot again. Macro is iterative.

step.name: Edit lightly, sharpen selectively
step.text: Adjust white balance, lift contrast slightly, and sharpen only the eyes or key feature. Avoid heavy saturation. Crop gently, not to re-compose.
